| P-002 | [Erdős–Gyárfás Conjecture](erdos-gyarfas/STATE.md) | `active` | `open` | 2026-07-24 | TIER 1 (G015, the named deliverable): prove the cubic reduction — statement 0.1 holds iff it holds for cubic graphs. Route R1 first: prove no TIGHT 1-atom exists (a power-free graph whose unique sub-cubic vertex has degree exactly 2), or find one (which disproves 0.1 outright by L025 R4). Do NOT work the unrestricted 1-atom question: L036 proves it is conjecture-complete (counterexample + one pendant vertex = a 1-atom), which is why sixteen sessions deferred it and why L029 had to be restated with tightness. Concrete first move: derive the forced local structure at the degree-2 vertex of a minimum-order tight 1-atom (its two neighbours, the C4-free neighbourhood layers, whether the L030 middle-layer collapse and the L033 pencil dichotomy transfer from the two-terminal to the one-terminal object), with base data C027 (none through order 15) and C034 (no bipartite tight 1-atom through order 22). Route R2 held as the alternative: verify Carr's argument that >=4/7 of a minimal counterexample is cubic (C006), then attempt 4/7 -> 1, which gives the same theorem without the atom formalism; ingredients on hand are C005 and A012 T4's unconditional branch. Also open and unrepaired: A012 Remark T4.1 is withdrawn (its minimality argument fails when the pendant-reduct is a cubic counterexample). TIER 3 background legs — run them, never select them as a session's primary work: order-23 bipartite hunt (E015; order 22 completed empty, 178,549 in class, splits (11,12) and (10,13) remain, a few hours); the odd-prime-gcd scan over the existing order-<=16 stream (needs real path enumeration; the bipartite shortcut does not apply); order-17+ pinched catalogue legs; census mining. TIER 2 alternatives if Tier 1 stalls: bipartite EGC as a theorem (L035 fixes the class, C034 gives the base through 22, and the naive 'C8 always' form is false at large order via bipartite double covers of C009's large-girth cubic graphs), and the path-spectrum gcd theorem (is gcd(S) always a power of 2 under condition (D)? Theta(3,3,3) shows the degree condition must do the work; A016 M10's 3-connected cycle analogue is the proved cousin). TIER 4: the dedicated {C4,C8}-free generator, which unlocks cubic order 30 and bipartite order 31 at once. PROCESS RULE (S018): every strategy audit must name the tier its selected route serves; a Tier 3 leg may not be chosen as primary work on cheapness alone. |
